title: The Coronation at Óbidos
title_candidates: Fourth Time's the Charm: Jon Baker's Double
From Green Jacket to Wooden Spoon
theme: Jon Baker, three TEGs of fourth-place purgatory behind him, finally converts a Lisbon week into the double — Trophy and Jacket — while the reigning Green Jacket holder Gregg Williams suffers the most thorough inversion in the field: from back-to-back Jackets to a first-ever Wooden Spoon, courtesy of Alex Baker handing the cutlery on.
opening_hook: Open at Royal Óbidos, R3, the 16th green: Jon Baker walks off having just signed for an 86 — a new course record on Seve's last design — with the Trophy lead at eleven and the Jacket lead growing by the hole. Then loop back to who he had been before this week (4th, 4th, 4th).
foreshadow: Jon Baker's last three Trophy finishes — 4th, 4th, 4th — and a reputation as the perennial nearly-man
Gregg Williams arriving as reigning Green Jacket holder, on back-to-back Jacket wins, with only one Spoon to his name in nine prior TEGs
Alex Baker carrying back-to-back Wooden Spoons and looking for someone — anyone — to take it off him
John Patterson's R1 lead — a single point clear after eighteen holes at Praia D'El Rey

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Patterson held the overnight R1 lead by a single point; Jon Baker seized it back at R2 H1 and was never headed across the next 56 holes. The cushion was 2 after R2, 11 after R3 (built largely on his 20-point burst across holes 11–16 at Royal Óbidos), and 18 at the close — finishing on 166, the 9th-best Trophy total of all time.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Five lead changes in the first 42 holes — Williams led after R1, Mullin after a course-record 82 at West Cliffs, then Mullin briefly outright at R3 H6 — before Jon Baker took it back at R3 H7 and ran. His own course-record 86 at Royal Óbidos in the same round opened an 8-shot lead; he closed +67, winning by 13, despite two quadruple-bogey 9s in the final round.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Alex Baker, the reigning Spoon holder, sat bottom after R1 (cushioned by a 10 at the 5th and a 9 at the 18th). Patterson took the basement at West Cliffs after bleeding 27 shots across holes 8–15. Williams then collapsed at Royal Óbidos — bottom of the round on 26, dropping into last outright at R3 H14, and bleeding 14 shots across the closing seven. He never climbed off the floor.

headline_candidates: Patterson Edges a Day of Quadruples
rounds.chosen_headline: A Single Point Clear at Praia
rounds.angle: Opening day at Praia D'El Rey, the area's fifth TEG visit. Patterson noses one point ahead on Stableford despite a quadruple at the 1st; Williams leads the Jacket on a 93; Mullin's back nine surge hints at what's coming. Alex Baker's 114 sets an unwanted course record on a venue with 24 prior visits.

headline_candidates: Mullin Breaks West Cliffs; Patterson Breaks Down
Two Course Records at the Cliffs — In Both Directions
rounds.chosen_headline: Two Course Records at the Cliffs — In Both Directions
rounds.angle: Cynthia Dye's West Cliffs, second TEG visit, delivers its best and worst in the same round: Mullin's 82 lowers the record by six, Patterson's 116 raises the ceiling by one. Between them Jon Baker quietly takes the Trophy lead at H1 and never lets it go; Alex Baker posts a personal best 105 here and slips off the basement.

Royal Óbidos, Ballesteros's final design, fourth TEG visit. Jon Baker shoots 86 — a course record on 18 prior visits — and a six-hole burst (11–16) yields 20 Stableford points. Mullin briefly takes the Jacket outright at H6 before Baker takes it back at H7. At the other end, Williams' 14-shot bleed across holes 12–18 drops him into the Spoon position at H14, and he stays there.

headline_candidates: Jon Baker Wobbles, Wins by Eighteen
rounds.angle: Back to Praia D'El Rey for the sixth TEG visit. The leads — 11 on Trophy, 8 on Jacket — were never threatened. Baker himself ran up two quadruple-bogey 9s on holes 10 and 17 (the latter on the course's SI 1) and still won both prizes pulling up. Patterson signs off with sextuples at 14 and 15; Alex Baker bleeds 25 shots across nine holes but doesn't need to.

players.arc: Three straight TEGs spent finishing 4th on the Trophy — finally turns Lisbon into the double, a course record at Royal Óbidos, and 18-shot and 13-shot margins.
Runner-up in both Trophy and Jacket; his 82 at West Cliffs is the round of the week but it's the only round he leads the Jacket, and even that lasts a hole.
Reigning Green Jacket holder and four-time Trophy winner; leaves with only his second Wooden Spoon after a R3 collapse at Royal Óbidos.
Overnight R1 leader after eighteen holes; a 27-shot West Cliffs implosion and a course-worst 116 in the same round end the brief flirtation with the front.
Carrying back-to-back Spoons in; sets a Praia D'El Rey course-worst on day one, but a personal-best 105 at West Cliffs is enough to pass the cutlery on to Williams.

venue_notes: Sixth TEG visit to the Lisbon Coast across four rounds. R1 and R4 at Praia D'El Rey (Cabell Robinson, links-meets-parkland; 5th and 6th visits — used as bookends). R2 at Cynthia Dye's West Cliffs (2nd visit; both course records broken in this round, in opposite directions). R3 at Royal Óbidos — Seve's last design, 4th visit — the dramatic centrepiece where Jon Baker rewrites the course record on the round that decides everything. Treat Royal Óbidos as the act-two pivot.
course_history_notes: Jon Baker's new PB at Royal Óbidos (86, beating his own 89 from TEG 9) is the course record
Jon Baker's new PB at West Cliffs (86, 15 shots better than TEG 15) — his second new PB of the week
David Mullin's new PB at West Cliffs (82, 12 shots better than TEG 15) — also the course record
Alex Baker's new PB at West Cliffs (105, 5 shots better than TEG 15) — the basement-dweller's bright spot
John Patterson's new PB at Royal Óbidos (94, 6 shots better than TEG 9) — buried in a week of trouble
Gregg Williams seven shots better than his last visit to Praia D'El Rey in R1, then unravels
decisive_moments: Trophy: R2 H1 at West Cliffs — Jon Baker takes the outright lead from Patterson and is never headed across the next 56 holes
Green Jacket: R3 H7 at Royal Óbidos — Jon Baker takes the Jacket lead back from Mullin (who had held it outright for one hole) and pulls clear with the course-record 86
Wooden Spoon: R3 H14 at Royal Óbidos — Gregg Williams drops outright to the bottom and stays there for the remaining 22 holes

payoffs.resolves_in: Act III / Round 3 / Trophy and Jacket spines
payoffs.payoff: Wins both Trophy (by 18) and Green Jacket (by 13) — 9th-best Trophy total of all time, third Jacket

payoffs.seed: Gregg Williams as reigning Jacket holder on back-to-back wins
payoffs.payoff: From defending Jacket to his second-ever Wooden Spoon — the inversion of the week
His West Cliffs PB and Williams's R3 collapse pass the cutlery on; the streak ends at two
Lost at R2 H1 and never threatened again; by R2 H10 he's at the bottom himself, eventually finishing the round with a course-worst 116 at West Cliffs
